content: Anticoagulants prevent thrombus formation by inhibiting coagulation cascade. Warfarin: vitamin K antagonist, inhibits synthesis of factors II, VII, IX, X and proteins C, S. Requires INR monitoring (target 2.0-3.0 for most indications, 2.5-3.5 for mechanical mitral valve). Many drug-drug and drug-food interactions (CYP2C9 metabolism, vitamin K intake). DOACs/NOACs (direct oral anticoagulants): dabigatran (direct thrombin inhibitor), rivaroxaban/apixaban/edoxaban (factor Xa inhibitors). Fixed dosing, no routine monitoring, fewer interactions, rapid onset/offset. Renal clearance varies: dabigatran 80%, edoxaban 50%, rivaroxaban 33%, apixaban 25%—apixaban safest in CKD. Indications: atrial fibrillation stroke prevention, VTE treatment and prevention, post-orthopedic surgery prophylaxis. Contraindications: mechanical heart valves (use warfarin), moderate-severe mitral stenosis with AF (warfarin), severe renal impairment (varies by agent), active bleeding, antiphospholipid syndrome (warfarin preferred).
